title: Combinational Circuits: Adders
summary: Build arithmetic circuits from logic gates — half adder, full adder, and ripple carry adder.
content_html:
A combinational circuit is a network of logic gates where outputs depend only on current inputs (no memory). The simplest arithmetic circuits are adders.

Half Adder

Adds two 1-bit inputs (A, B), producing a Sum and Carry:

Sum   = A XOR B  (A⊕B)
Carry = A AND B  (A.B)
ABSumCarry
0000
0110
1010
1101

Full Adder

Extends the half adder to include a carry-in (Cin):

Sum   = A XOR B XOR Cin
Cout  = (A AND B) OR (Cin AND (A XOR B))

Ripple Carry Adder

Chain multiple full adders together — the carry-out of each stage connects to the carry-in of the next, building an n-bit adder.

Common Errors

  • Confusing half adder with full adder — the full adder handles carry-in
  • Overflow: when the result exceeds the available bit width

exercises.title: Half adder sum
exercises.prompt: What is the Sum output of a half adder when both A=1 and B=1?

exercises.title: Half adder carry
exercises.prompt: What is the Carry output of a half adder when A=1 and B=1?

exercises.title: Sum gate
exercises.prompt: The Sum output of a half adder is implemented using which gate?

exercises.title: Carry gate
exercises.prompt: The Carry output of a half adder is implemented using which gate?

exercises.title: Full vs half
exercises.prompt: What does a full adder have that a half adder does not?

exercises.title: Ripple carry
exercises.prompt: In a ripple carry adder, what connects each full adder stage?
options: The carry-out of one stage to carry-in of the next
The sum output to the next input
exercises.answer: The carry-out of one stage to carry-in of the next

exercises.title: Combinational definition
exercises.prompt: A combinational circuit's output depends on:

exercises.title: 0+1 half adder
exercises.prompt: Half adder: A=0, B=1. What are Sum and Carry?

exercises.title: Full adder Cin
exercises.prompt: Full adder: A=1, B=1, Cin=1. What is the Sum?
